message:
feat(tracker): add severity classification and LastAt tracking for conflicts

This commit enhances conflict detection with intelligent severity classification
and improved timing information for better alerting and prioritization.

Conflict Struct Enhancement:
- Add LastAt field to track the most recent change timestamp
- Enables sorting and filtering by recency

Severity Classification (conflictSeverity function):
- "critical" if 3+ agents touched the same file
  (high probability of merge conflicts or coordination issues)
- "critical" if edits occurred within a 10-minute window
  (indicates active simultaneous editing)
- "warning" for all other multi-agent file access
  (standard awareness alert)

DetectConflicts Improvements:
- Refactored condition logic for clarity (early continue instead of nested if)
- Track LastAt by iterating through pathChanges timestamps
- Calculate severity dynamically using conflictSeverity()

Rationale:
Previously all conflicts were flagged as "warning" regardless of risk level.
The new classification helps operators:
- Prioritize critical conflicts that need immediate attention
- Distinguish between active conflicts (10min window) and historical overlap
- Identify coordination breakdowns (3+ agents on same file)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
